attribution.text: Route, junction, and history details adapted from the Wikipedia article "Hells Canyon Scenic Byway", by Wikipedia contributors, licensed under CC BY-SA 4.0.

sources.name: Federal Highway Administration — National Scenic Byways

paragraphs: The Hells Canyon Scenic Byway was designated a National Forest Scenic Byway on April 19, 1992. It was later made an Oregon State Scenic Byway on February 19, 1997. The All-American Road designation was applied to the roadway on June 15, 2000.

references.text: Staff. "Hells Canyon Scenic Byway". National Scenic Byways. Federal Highway Administration. Retrieved May 13, 2008.
Staff. "Official Designations". Hells Canyon Scenic Byway. Federal Highway Administration. Retrieved September 11, 2011.

gallery.desc: A frozen creek meanders through a snowy gap between frosted pine trees in the Hells Canyon Scenic Byway region. Location: Location: Oregon (45.315° N 116.837° W) Status: Public domain.
A snowmobiler carves brand-new tracks into deep powder on a hillside along Hells Canyon Scenic Byway. Location: Location: Oregon (45.345° N 116.995° W) Status: Public domain.
A group of snowmobile riders takes a break on a forest's groomed pathway, showing off their sleds on an ideal snow day. Location: Location: Oregon (45.277° N 116.955° W) Status: Public domain.
A snowmobiler maneuvers at high speed through deep powder near Hells Canyon Scenic Byway. Location: Location: Oregon (45.337° N 117.003° W) Status: Public domain.
The river winds through the depths of Hells Canyon, providing water to ther verdant wilderness. Location: Location: Oregon (45.494° N 116.554° W) Status: Public domain.

gallery.cap: Pioneer Historic Byway Carved into the Earth
gallery.desc: Hells Canyon Reservoir carves its way through the landscape near Sheep Rock. Evergreens fill one riverbank; sloping gray rocks form the other. Location: Hells Canyon Reservoir, Idaho (45.143° N 116.729° W) Status: Public domain. Photo taken by Tony Varilone
The calm waters of Hells Canyon Reservoir are overlooked by cliffs and tall green trees. Location: Hells Canyon Dam, Idaho (45.237° N 116.705° W) Status: Public domain. Photo taken by Tony Varilone

gallery.cap: Pioneer Historic Byway Hells Canyon Dam
gallery.desc: This dam for Hells Canyon Reservoir regulates the water. Mountains form one side of the river and the white power plant stands beside the dam. Location: Hells Canyon Dam, Idaho (45.244° N 116.701° W) Status: Public domain. Photo taken by Tony Varilone

gallery.cap: Pioneer Historic Byway Hells Canyon Reservoir
gallery.desc: Hells Canyon Reservoir flows peacefully around the base of the rocks. Wildflowers are blooming in the foreground. Location: Hells Canyon Reservoir, Idaho (45.237° N 116.703° W) Status: Public domain. Photo taken by Tony Varilone
The sky reflects this water at Hells Canyon Dam. Tall evergreen trees fill the riverbank. Location: Hells Canyon Dam, Idaho (45.241° N 116.702° W) Status: Public domain. Photo taken by Tony Varilone

gallery.cap: Pioneer Historic Byway Rearview of Hells Canyon Dam
gallery.desc: Deep green pines hang over the edges of the green waters. A white building of the dam's power plant stands at the reservoir's far end. Location: Hells Canyon Dam, Idaho (45.241° N 116.702° W) Status: Public domain. Photo taken by Tony Varilone

The pines along the edge of this river stand majestically as the water flows through the land near Sheep Rock. Location: Hells Canyon Reservoir, Idaho (45.264° N 116.691° W) Status: Public domain. Photo taken by Tony Varilone

The pines atop this mountain are reflected in the shimmering waters of the reservoir formed by Hells Canyon Dam. Location: Hells Canyon Dam, Idaho (45.235° N 116.704° W) Status: Public domain. Photo taken by Tony Varilone

gallery.cap: Pioneer Historic Byway Winding Down Hells Canyon
gallery.desc: Hells Canyon winds along the base of the mountain from this rocky vantage point above the river. Location: Hells Canyon Reservoir, Idaho (44.984° N 116.827° W) Status: Public domain. Photo taken by Tony Varilone

gallery.cap: Pioneer Historic Byway Pasture Fences and Blue Skies
gallery.desc: A pasture fence frames the blue waters of the Hells Canyon Reservoir. Blue skies look down upon the scene. Location: Hells Canyon Dam, Idaho (43.004° N 111.714° W) Status: Public domain. Photo taken by Tony Varilone

Halloween in La Grande Oregon’s Award Winning Historic Downtown Celebrating Halloween at the downtown trick or treat in downtown La Grande Located in the heart of Northeast Oregon in the Grande Ronde Valley, the City of La Grande is surrounded by the Wallowa’s and the Blue Mountains and is the southern starting and ending point for the Hells Canyon Scenic Byway. La Grande’s award-winning downtown
gallery.credit: La Grande Oregon Economic Development · CC BY 2.0
